title: First sessions: goals, corrections, and building the site
summary: Set learning goals, learned 8 key rules (talk with/to, want + to + verb, make a mistake, every + singular, whether), and built this study website.

title: Preposition + -ing
summary: After a preposition, use the -ing form (good at speaking). New words: fluent, awkward, get along with, come up with, eventually, plus two conversation phrases.

title: Giving opinions
summary: Learned how to express opinions: I think, I believe, In my opinion, I'd say, From my perspective. New words: perspective, argue, reasonable, convince, tend to.

title: used to — past habits
summary: Learned 'used to + verb' for past habits no longer done, and how it differs from 'be used to + -ing'. New words: routine, lately, struggle, expand, confident. Two expressions on habits and recent progress.

title: Giving advice and suggestions
summary: Learned four ways to give advice and make suggestions: should, Why don't you/we, How about + -ing, and You might want to. New words: suggest, recommend, encourage, remind, overcome.

title: Present perfect
summary: Learned 'have/has + past participle' for experiences (I've visited Japan), recent events (I've just finished), and ongoing situations (I've studied English for five years). New words: accomplish, achieve, progress, keep up with, click.

title: 2nd conditional: hypothetical 'what if'
summary: Learned the 2nd conditional (If + past simple, would + base verb) for unreal/imaginary situations. Key rule: never put 'would' in the if-clause. New words: afford, immerse, abroad, dedicate, pick up.

title: Future plans: going to / planning to / intending to
summary: Learned when to use 'be going to' (pre-decided plans) vs 'will' (spontaneous decisions). Also: planning to, thinking of + -ing, intending to. New words: intend, arrange, look forward to, definitely, upcoming.

title: Concession: although / even though vs. despite / in spite of
summary: Learned to contrast two ideas using although/even though + clause vs. despite/in spite of + noun/-ing. Key rule: 'despite' is a preposition, never use it before a full clause. New words: despite, occasionally, whereas, regardless, obstacle.

title: Relative clauses: who / which / that
summary: Learned to add description to nouns using relative clauses: 'who' for people, 'which' for things, 'that' for either. Key rule: never use 'what' as a relative pronoun; never add an extra pronoun inside the clause. New words: mention, impress, specific, relate to, plenty of.

title: Reported speech: say / tell + (that)
summary: Learned how to report what others said using say (that) and tell + person + (that), with tense backshift: present→past, will→would, can→could. Key rule: 'say' never takes a person object — use 'tell' instead. New words: report, inform, claim, deny, admit.

title: Passive voice: be + past participle
summary: Learned to form the passive voice (subject + be + past participle) to focus on the action rather than the doer. Key rule: use the past participle, never the simple past, after 'be' (written not wrote). New words: manufacture, broadcast, hire, publish, replace.
